description: Bundled PT6A-27 cards for free-turbine architecture, NG/NP, flat rating, takeoff power and engine limits terminology.

cards.front: What does PT6A-27 mean in the Twin Otter context?
cards.back: PT stands for propeller turbine, 6 is the engine family, A denotes the two-stage gearbox configuration, and -27 is the engine series.
There is no mechanical coupling between the gas generator section and the power section.
Gas generator speed, expressed as a percentage of 37,500 RPM. It is directly controlled by the power lever through the fuel control unit.
Propeller speed, expressed as a percentage of 33,000 RPM. It is controlled with the propeller lever via the propeller governor.

cards.front: What is the DHC-6 PT6A-27 flat rating?
cards.back: The PT6A-27 was designed for 680 SHP, but on the DHC-6 it is flat rated to 620 HP, equivalent to 50 PSI torque.
The approved takeoff power setting calculated from the chart/torque computer and set without reduction for every takeoff.

cards.front: What propeller RPM is required to achieve maximum takeoff power?
cards.back: The propellers must be set to maximum RPM, 96% NP.

cards.front: For the PT6A-27 on the DHC-6, how does maximum continuous power compare to maximum takeoff power?
cards.back: Maximum continuous power is the same as maximum takeoff power, with no time restriction, but it is for abnormal/emergency conditions requiring absolute maximum engine performance.
A start with T5 greater than 1090°C at any time, greater than 980°C for more than 2 seconds, or greater than 925°C for more than 10 seconds.
The aft-most power lever position without twisting the grips to overcome the mechanical barrier; moving aft of the barrier in flight is prohibited.
